description: Fix flaky file I/O patterns in E2E tests: replace direct fs.writeFileSync with writeAndWaitForWatcher() in hot-reload-dynamic tests, and replace fs.readFileSync polling of config.json with MCP-based state checks in side-panel-plugin-toggle tests.

userStories.title: Use writeAndWaitForWatcher() for manifest writes in hot-reload-dynamic multi-session and reload tests
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want file-watcher writes in hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts to use the retry-on-miss pattern so that tests do not flake on macOS FSEvents timing.
acceptanceCriteria: Lines ~1156 and ~1198 in e2e/hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts wrap writeToolsToManifest() calls inside writeAndWaitForWatcher(), matching the pattern used elsewhere in the same file (e.g., lines 322, 368, 667, 876, 1086)
No direct writeToolsToManifest() calls remain outside writeAndWaitForWatcher() in tests that depend on file-watcher detection
The surrounding test logic (waitForToolList polling) still functions correctly after the change

userStories.title: Replace fs.readFileSync config polling with MCP-based state checks in side-panel-plugin-toggle tests
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts to verify config persistence via MCP state rather than reading config.json from disk, so that tests do not fail from partial file reads during concurrent writes.
acceptanceCriteria: The expect.poll() blocks at lines ~177-187, ~208-218, and ~420-429 in e2e/side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts no longer use fs.readFileSync to read config.json
Config persistence is verified via MCP-based checks (e.g., mcpClient.listTools() to confirm tool presence/absence, or polling /health endpoint for plugin state)
The test still verifies the same functional behavior: that toggling a tool in the side panel persists the change and removes/restores the tool from MCP tools/list
